insight: Scorchilla usage depends on opponent's Blastforge availability: build it first if they lack Forge, defer if they have Forge. It functions primarily as a tech-out tool and threat rather than immediate damage, with 5RR for 1 attack being efficient due to the implied damage threat.
author: leary93
context: Discussion of how to use Scorchilla effectively in early game sequencing.

insight: Against Double Doomed Engineer (DDE) on turn 2, viable responses include: natural Conduit if aggressive green units present, fast Animus if available, or aggressive red units like Grimbotch/Vore/Scorchilla. Green-blue units like Tesla, Plasmafier, and Redeemer can also punish hard but are harder to execute.
author: leary93
context: Advice on punishing opponent's economic play with DDE.

insight: Fast Animus sequences typically use 10-13 drones and follow patterns like DD DA DTT (double drone, drone animus, drone tech tech) or DDT, possibly extending to 13 drones with Blastforge for walls. Speed of Animus determines drone count—if fast Animus is viable, you won't have time to build many drones.
author: consensus
context: Discussion of fast Animus opening patterns and drone count optimization.

insight: Odin Matrix interaction: when Odin is unclicked (not activated), it functions as defense; clicking Odin removes Steelsplitter supply. Clicking Odin to remove opponent's vigilant blockers (e.g., Sentries) can be worthwhile if you're not sacrificing additional defense purchases, but holding Odin is often superior due to tempo loss from clicking.
author: consensus
context: Extended debate on optimal Odin Matrix usage and whether clicking Odin is efficient.

insight: Egg Turbine has a superior durability-to-gold-income ratio (2.0) compared to other drones like Thorium Dynamo (1.6), Trinity Drone (1.67), Wild Drone (1.5), and Vivid Drone (0.33), making Base + Egg Turbine + Fabrication potentially stronger breach-proof than Base + Trinity + Fabrication.
author: shadourow
context: Analysis of drone cost-efficiency ratios for breach-proof strategies.

insight: Apollo's effectiveness is highly dependent on defensive options available in the set, not primarily on offensive unit choices. With high-quality soak like Plexo Cell or Polywall plus strong absorbers, Apollo becomes nearly unbeatable with cheap (<3 HP) attackers.
author: shadourow, namington
context: Analysis of what makes Apollo-based strategies viable or unviable.

insight: Against Apollo, sniping Perforators is inefficient (worse value than sniping Tarsiers), but sniping Animus can be valuable if it disrupts opponent's red consumption or Iceblade purchases, though other blue attackers typically provide better value.
author: namington, shadourow
context: Discussion of target prioritization for Apollo in mixed unit sets.

insight: Cynestra should almost never be purchased when Apollo is still viable as an opponent option, as Apollo will snipe Cynestra for value. Only viable if opponent is forced to commit away from Apollo or if you can stall Apollo one turn while pivoting Cynestra the next.
author: namington
context: Strategic analysis of Cynestra viability in Apollo-containing sets.

insight: Xaetron with Matrix present: prefer absorbing on Matrix every turn and clicking Xaetron rather than alternating absorbs. Xaetron's click value (~2.75 damage/turn) exceeds defense value when you're already absorbing >3 per turn, making it function more like a bursty attacker than an absorber.
author: amalloy
context: Technical analysis of Xaetron damage calculation and optimal play with Matrix.

insight: Xaetron's charge mechanics: can fire twice in a row around turn 8-9 of holding it (assuming purchase turn is turn 1). Charges have Exhaust 1 property, making them similar in speed to Tarsier (Build Time 1).
author: consensus
context: Clarification of Xaetron charge timing and mechanics.

insight: Doomed Drone supply is limited and valuable. With 2 Energy available, always buy Doomed Drone. With 4 Energy, buy 2 Doomed Drones. With 3 Energy, prefer regular Drone in most inflation levels due to supply scarcity, unless in high inflation.
author: consensus
context: Optimization of Doomed Drone purchasing decisions based on energy availability and game state.

insight: Tia Threnody is a 'always wins' unit if achieved, but getting it may be too costly economically. Viable path is Animus → Tia if already have 3 green and not spending all resources on defense, but early Doomed Mech and Blood Pact defend against early Tia attempts.
author: crash_overlord
context: Discussion of Tia Threnody viability and tech paths in complex sets.

insight: Immolite vs Tarsier: Tarsier is superior in long games, but Immolite's faster hit is better in endgame or when immediate attack is critical (e.g., games ending in 3 turns or fewer). Immolite also better when forced to spend all available red (6 gold) or when opponent must make difficult defensive decisions.
author: consensus
context: Comparison of attack ramp speeds and situational viability of Immolite vs Tarsier.

insight: In base set, Fast Animus (DD/DA for P2) is the standard winning line. Slow Animus (DD/DD/DDA as P2) is generally inefficient and should be avoided, with rare exceptions like Lucina.
author: shadourow
context: Baseline opening theory for base set economic sequencing.

insight: Militia is significantly better than Steelsplitter in absorb-denial scenarios: 2-3 Militias exceed Splitter value, especially with additional absorb denial or higher absorb in the set. Splitter is only better with Odin and Manticore, or versus pure red strategies.
author: awaclus, leary93
context: Analysis of Militia effectiveness as absorb denial versus conventional Splitter.

insight: Xeno Guardian and Urban Sentry subsequent copies are less valuable than the first if threatened damage is low, similar to Wall comparison. Urban Sentry is particularly good because it absorbs for 2; multiple Sentries work best against chill/threat when opponent is forced to spread damage.
author: awaclus, leary93
context: Discussion of diminishing returns on buying multiple copies of threat-absorbing units.

insight: Arka (Tatsu Nullifier) should generally be held rather than clicked for attack unless: overdefended with tempo surplus, can kill vigilant blockers to justify the defense tempo loss, or have no other blue sink available. Holding Arka trades 4 absorb half-turn later for maintaining 7 defense tempo now.
author: awaclus, leary93, crash_overlord
context: Extended debate on Arka clicking vs holding and tempo trade-offs.

insight: Clicking Arka is reasonable if: you have 10BB+ with nothing else to spend on besides walls, are overdefended by exactly 1, and can kill vigilant blockers to justify tempo loss. It should not be pursued at cost of buying additional defensive walls.
author: mrguy888
context: Specific conditions for reasonable Arka clicking.
